2604.04563.title: Temporal Inversion for Learning Interval Change in Chest X-Rays
2604.04563.paper_summary: Recent advances in vision--language pretraining have enabled strong medical foundation models, yet most analyze radiographs in isolation, overlooking the key clinical task of comparing prior and current images to assess interval change. For chest radiographs (CXRs), capturing interval change is essential, as radiologists must evaluate not only the static appearance of findings but also how they evolve over time. We introduce TILA (Temporal Inversion-aware Learning and Alignment), a simple yet effective framework that uses temporal inversion, reversing image pairs, as a supervisory signal to enhance the sensitivity of existing temporal vision-language models to directional change. TILA integrates inversion-aware objectives across pretraining, fine-tuning, and inference, complementing conventional appearance modeling with explicit learning of temporal order. We also propose a unified evaluation protocol to assess order sensitivity and consistency under temporal inversion, and introduce MS-CXR-Tretrieval, a retrieval evaluation set constructed through a general protocol that can be applied to any temporal CXR dataset. Experiments on public datasets and real-world hospital cohorts demonstrate that TILA consistently improves progression classification and temporal embedding alignment when applied to multiple existing architectures.
2604.04563.paper_summary_zh: 最近在視覺-語言預訓練方面的進展使得強大的醫療基礎模型成為可能，然而大多數模型僅孤立分析放射影像，忽略了比較先前和當前影像以評估時間變化這一關鍵臨床任務。對於胸部放射影像（CXRs），捕捉時間變化是至關重要的，因為放射科醫生必須評估發現的靜態外觀以及它們隨時間的演變。我們介紹了 TILA（時間反轉感知學習與對齊），這是一個簡單但有效的框架，利用時間反轉，即反轉影像對，作為監督信號，以增強現有時間視覺-語言模型對方向性變化的敏感性。TILA 在預訓練、微調和推理過程中整合了反轉感知目標，通過明確學習時間順序來補充傳統的外觀建模。我們還提出了一個統一的評估協議，以評估在時間反轉下的順序敏感性和一致性，並介紹了 MS-CXR-Tretrieval，這是一個通過通用協議構建的檢索評估集，可以應用於任何時間 CXR 數據集。在公共數據集和現實世界醫院隊列上的實驗表明，當 TILA 應用於多個現有架構時，能夠穩定地改善進展分類和時間嵌入對齊。
2604.04563.author: Hanbin Ko et.al.
2604.04563.authors: Hanbin Ko, Kyungmin Jeon, Doowoong Choi, Chang Min Park
